findings.title: Disabled failover still resets atomics on every URMA result
findings.impact: When `urma_failover_success_rate_ratio` is set to `0.0`, every Set/Get URMA result still calls `RecordUrmaResult` and executes `ResetWindow()`, which performs several atomic stores on the foreground data path. Operators disabling this feature to avoid failover overhead still pay synchronization cost at request frequency.
findings.fix: Make the disabled path a cheap no-op after the first transition, for example by tracking an enabled/disabled generation or only clearing the window when the config changes from enabled to disabled.
